title: Dead-letter after bounded attempts
description: A message that keeps failing is routed to a dead-letter queue after a max-attempt count instead of redelivering forever.
body: Use when consuming from a queue where a single bad message could fail repeatedly. Do not let an unprocessable (poison) message redeliver indefinitely and block the consumer. Actions: track delivery/attempt count, after the cap move the message to a dead-letter queue with the error and context, alert, and continue with the next message. Validation signals include a test that a permanently failing message lands in the DLQ after N attempts and does not stall the queue. Risk boundary: no DLQ means one poison message can halt all processing; treat as a blocking design gap.
